pageCaption.value: Russian-born choreographer Alexei Ratmansky and his protégé Myles Thatcher share a deep respect for the “language” that is classical dance. Both are intensely involved in reinvigorating their art form for the 21st century. A perfect partnership.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Dance, 2014–2015

text.value: Russian-born choreographer Alexei Ratmansky and his protégé Myles Thatcher share a deep respect for the “language” that is classical dance. Both are intensely involved in reinvigorating their art form for the 21st century. A perfect partnership.

pageTitle.value: Lin Hwai-min and Eduardo Fukushima - Video

pageCaption.value: Young Brazilian dancer Eduardo Fukushima moves from São Paulo to Taiwan, where his mentor, Asia’s premier choreographer Lin Hwai-min, elaborates his unique philosophy of movement and being. A year with this “very generous person” has “transformed my life”, Fukushima declares.

WaPageTitle.value: Lin Hwai-min and Eduardo Fukushima - Video

metadescription.value: Young Brazilian dancer Eduardo Fukushima moves from São Paulo to Taiwan, where his mentor, Asia’s premier choreographer Lin Hwai-min, elaborates his unique philosophy of movement and being. A year with this “very generous person” has “transformed my life”, Fukushima declares.

previewTitle.value: Lin Hwai-min and Eduardo Fukushima - Video

pageTitle.value: William Forsythe and Sang Jijia - Video

pageCaption.value: William Forsythe, one of the greatest innovators of modern dance, believes that ballet “shouldn’t be left in the 19th century”. His radical approach finds the perfect protégé in Sang Jijia, a gifted Chinese dancer of Tibetan origin. “Sang-ba is like unpolluted water,” says Forsythe. “He runs clear.”

WaPageTitle.value: William Forsythe and Sang Jijia - Video

pageTitle.value: Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker and Anani Dodji Sanouvi - Video

pageCaption.value: Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker, one of the world’s most distinguished choreographers, says of her protégé, Togolese dancer Anani Dodji Sanouvi, “he is like the sun … he has an energy that is inspiring.” Sanouvi in turn declares that a year being mentored by De Keersmaeker is the chance of a lifetime.

WaPageTitle.value: Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker and Anani Dodji Sanouvi - Video

pageTitle.value: Alejandro G. Iñárritu and Tom Shoval - Video

pageCaption.value: Young Israeli director Tom Shoval’s year of mentoring evolved with the excitement worthy of a Hollywood film script. Shoval spent weeks on the set of Alejandro G. Iñárritu’s film 'The Revenant' with the Oscar winner revealing all the “infinite possibilities” of film-making.

WaPageTitle.value: Alejandro G. Iñárritu and Tom Shoval - Video

pageTitle.value: Michael Ondaatje and Miroslav Penkov - Video

pageCaption.value: Bulgarian protégé Miroslav Penkov could not believe that his mentor was Michael Ondaatje, “someone who stands so tall in a field of giants”. For Ondaatje, author of The English Patient, “our lives are utterly disordered”, so watching Penkov bring order into life by writing fiction was a source of pleasure.

WaPageTitle.value: Michael Ondaatje and Miroslav Penkov - Video

pageTitle.value: Kaija Saariaho and Vasco Mendonça - Video

pageCaption.value: Composing music is a solitary occupation. For Finland’s Kaija Saariaho and her protégé, Portugal’s Vasco Mendonça the mentorship delivered a rare opportunity to “enrich” each other discussing music in concert halls around Europe and in the United States.
